assignments.name: allowedColors
assignments.value: blue, bleu, black, noir, white, blanc, multi, multicolor, multicolour, multicolore, red, rouge, yellow, jaune

parameters.jsCode: return $('Build Alert Batch').first().json.listings.map((listing) => ({ json: { workflowSlug: 'vinted-new-listing-alerts', itemKey: listing.itemKey } }));

parameters.content:
## Vinted new-listing alerts

Monitor one public Vinted search and receive Telegram alerts only for matching listings that have not been delivered before. The workflow uses `fetch_cat/vinted-search-scraper`, works on n8n Cloud or self-hosted n8n, and does not require OpenAI. The default is one hourly run returning 10 listings.

### How it works

The schedule or manual trigger creates state, validates your search, runs one focused FetchCat search per brand name (or one combined search with numeric brand IDs), deduplicates results, applies size and color filters, and checks the delivery ledger. The first run records a quiet baseline. Later runs send Telegram messages and commit IDs only after delivery succeeds.

### Setup

- [ ] Choose a frequency from 15 minutes to one day in `1. Choose Alert Frequency`.
- [ ] Enter the search, audience, Vinted domain, price range, optional filters, and 1-50 result limit in `2. Configure Vinted Search`.
- [ ] Connect an Apify HTTP Header Auth credential to both HTTP Request nodes.
- [ ] Connect Telegram and select your private destination in `4. Send New Listings to Telegram`.
- [ ] Test manually, verify the quiet baseline, then activate the workflow.

## Choose timing and create state

The default schedule is hourly. Edit the Schedule Trigger for 15 or 30 minutes, 6 or 12 hours, or once daily. Both Data Tables are created automatically and reused safely.

## Configure one saved search

Choose audience, brand, size, and color preferences. Size `M` matches `M / 38 / 10`. Color labels use listing titles; enable `requireColorInTitle` only for strict matching. Numeric IDs provide marketplace-side filtering.

## Run focused FetchCat searches

Each brand name creates a focused search; numeric brand IDs use one combined run. The result allowance is divided across searches, then listings are combined, deduplicated, capped, and filtered.

## Explain empty results

Matching listings continue to the delivery ledger. A valid zero-match search ends visibly with counts, the likely blocking filter, returned brands and sizes, and a practical next step.

## Keep unseen matches

Delivered IDs are removed. Remaining listings are batched and routed to a quiet first-run baseline or Telegram.

## Establish the first-run baseline

Existing result IDs are recorded, then the monitor is marked initialized. No Telegram message is sent. A failed ledger write leaves initialization incomplete so the run can be retried.

## Send readable Telegram alerts

New matches are grouped five per message with title, price, brand, size, condition, seller, engagement, and a direct link. IDs are committed only after every Telegram message succeeds, keeping failed deliveries retryable.

## Save monitor state

Initialization is written only after the baseline or Telegram delivery completes successfully.
